content.claim: Students from higher socioeconomic backgrounds are significantly more likely to attend early-selective academic tracks even when controlling for prior test scores, and mathematical and reading performance diverges between tracks even after accounting for skill and status selection.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Panel study methodology does not establish causation definitively
OECD finding on variance between schools does not isolate track selection from other school factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
